name: Upper limit on graviton mass from GW dispersion
requirement: Any candidate framework must predict a graviton mass m_g (or equivalent dispersion-modifying parameter) below the LIGO/Virgo GWTC-3 upper limit. A massive graviton would produce a frequency-dependent group velocity, dephasing the inspiral chirp from compact-binary mergers in a way LIGO/Virgo would have detected.

regime.description: Cosmological propagation of GW signals from binary black hole and neutron star mergers, frequencies 10-1000 Hz.

current_bound.experiment: LIGO/Virgo combined analysis of GWTC-3 events
current_bound.reference: Abbott et al. (LVC), 'Tests of General Relativity with GWTC-3', PRD 103, 122002 (2021), arXiv:2010.14529

what_it_excludes: Theories of massive gravity (dRGT, bigravity) with graviton mass above ~10^-23 eV. Combined with the GW170817 graviton-speed bound, severely constrains the parameter space of modified-gravity proposals for dark energy.

parameterization.name: Graviton dispersion: E^2 = p^2 c^2 + m_g^2 c^4. More general: parameterized GW dispersion with deviation parameter A_alpha at order p^alpha.
parameterization.reference: Mirshekari, Yunes, Will, PRD 85, 024041 (2012), arXiv:1110.2720; LVC GWTC-3 tests-of-GR analysis.
parameterization.summary: The standard parameterization adds a power-law modification to graviton dispersion. m_g is the alpha=0 case.

procedural.predicted_observable: Frequency-dependent group velocity v_g(f) = c sqrt(1 - (m_g c^2 / h f)^2) leading to inspiral phase shift Delta phi(f).
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: m_g < 1.27 x 10^-23 eV/c^2. For non-zero alpha, see GWTC-3 figure 4 and table 4 for bounds at alpha = 0, 0.5, 1, 1.5, ..., 4.
framework_inputs: Modified GW dispersion relation derived from the framework's tensor-perturbation EOM in vacuum.
Standard waveform-modeling pipeline (LALSuite) accepts the parameterized dispersion as a single deviation parameter.

primary_references.citation: Abbott et al. (LIGO/Virgo/KAGRA), 'Tests of General Relativity with GWTC-3', PRD 103, 122002 (2021)
de Rham, Deskins, Tolley, Zhou, 'Graviton Mass Bounds', RMP 89, 025004 (2017)
